info.title: Certbot Manager API
info.description:
A secure mTLS gateway for managing Certbot certificates via API.

---

## 🔐 Authentication Model (mTLS)

This API uses **mutual TLS (mTLS)**.

That means:
- The server verifies the client certificate
- The client must present a valid certificate during the TLS handshake

---

## 🌐 Using Swagger UI (this page)

Swagger UI can describe and send requests, but:

⚠️ It does NOT upload or manage client certificates.

To successfully call protected endpoints from this UI:
1. Your browser must already have the client certificate installed
2. The certificate must be trusted by the API server
3. The browser must present the certificate during connection

If this is not configured:
- Requests to protected endpoints will fail
- This is expected behavior

---

## 🧪 Recommended Testing (CLI)

Use curl or the provided script:

```bash
curl \
  --cacert ca.crt \
  --cert mtls-client-identity.crt \
  --key mtls-client-identity.key \
  https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/certs
```

Or:

```bash
./test-api.sh list
```

---

## 🧭 Suggested Flow

1. Call `/healthcheck` (no mTLS required)
2. Try protected endpoints in Swagger UI (may fail)
3. Copy curl example
4. Run via CLI or test-api.sh

---

servers.description: Cert Manager API (mTLS required for protected endpoints)

mutualTLS.description: Protected endpoints require a valid client certificate issued by the trusted client-auth CA. Swagger UI will only work if the browser is already configured with this certificate.

dns_provider.description: DNS provider used for ACME challenge

get.description:
Public endpoint to verify API availability. Does NOT require mTLS.

### Example
```bash
curl https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/healthcheck
```

Returns all configured certificates.

🔐 **mTLS required**

### CLI Example
```bash
curl \
  --cacert ca.crt \
  --cert mtls-client-identity.crt \
  --key mtls-client-identity.key \
  https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/certs
```

### Swagger UI Note
This will only work if your browser is already configured with a valid client certificate.

post.description:
Creates a new certificate entry.

🔐 **mTLS required**

### CLI Example
```bash
curl -X POST \
  --cacert ca.crt \
  --cert mtls-client-identity.crt \
  --key mtls-client-identity.key \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"fqdn":"test.example.com","dns_provider":"cloudflare","email":"admin@example.com"}' \
  https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/certs
```

delete.description:
Deletes a certificate entry.

🔐 **mTLS required**

### CLI Example
```bash
curl -X DELETE \
  --cacert ca.crt \
  --cert mtls-client-identity.crt \
  --key mtls-client-identity.key \
  "https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/certs?fqdn=test.example.com"
```

post.description:
Restarts the certbot-related container.

🔐 **mTLS required**

### CLI Example
```bash
curl -X POST \
  --cacert ca.crt \
  --cert mtls-client-identity.crt \
  --key mtls-client-identity.key \
  https://x.mydatacenter.io:8000/reload
```
